sections.title: From Words to Venn Diagrams
points: Many exam questions are written as stories about people or objects.
The first job is to turn the story into a Venn Diagram.
Look for keywords like 'only', 'at least one', and 'neither'.

sections.title: Only One Item
points: This means the members in one set only, not the overlap.
Example: Tea only or Cake only are the two outer regions of the diagram.
Add those regions to find the answer.

sections.title: At Least One
points: This asks for the union of the sets.
It includes both the only regions and the intersection.

sections.title: Neither or None
points: This is the outside region, not in either circle.
It is part of the Universal Set but not part of A or B.

sections.title: Probability in Venn Problems
points: Probability is target count divided by total count.
Always count all regions before writing the fraction.

cards.fact: A story that must be converted into set language.
cards.mnemonic: Read slowly and find the set clues.

cards.fact: The union of the sets, including the overlap.
cards.mnemonic: At least one means everyone who has anything.
Neither means none of the options.
Probability compares one part to the whole.
